---
title: Amberscript
categories: [Tools]
---

## What is Amberscript?

[Amberscript](https://www.amberscript.com/en/) is an online transcription service that converts audio and video recordings into text. It offers both automated speech-to-text transcription and human-edited transcription, allowing researchers to choose the level of accuracy and detail appropriate for their needs. The service is commonly used in research workflows to support the analysis, documentation, and reuse of audiovisual research data.

## Who is it for?

Amberscript can be used by researchers at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam who need to transcribe audio or video recordings as part of their research data workflow. Amberscript has a [blanket data processing agreement](/guides/comply-with-gdpr.html#agreements) in place with Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam. Researchers can use this service without having to arrange individual contracts. This enables them to upload and transcribe research data without setting up separate data processing agreements. As data protection requirements have already been addressed at the institutional level, transcription work can begin immediately.

## Transcription Options

Verbatim and literal transcription refer to **types of transcription** (the level of detail in the output), while automated versus human transcription refers to the **method** used to produce the transcript.

### Transcription Types and Methods

Amberscript offers different transcription types, allowing researchers to select the level of detail appropriate for their research purpose:

- **Verbatim transcription** - Cleaned-up text in which filler words, repetitions, and hesitations are removed.
- **Literal transcription** - Detailed transcription including hesitations, repetitions, and pauses; suitable for qualitative research and legal contexts.
- **Automated transcription** - Fast, software-based transcription that can be reviewed and edited by the researcher.

### Choosing the Appropriate Transcription Type and Method

The selection of transcription **type** and **method** depends on your research design, analytical goals, and available resources.

- When transcripts are **not the primary data** (e.g., used for reference or supplementary analysis), **automated transcription** or **verbatim transcription** is often sufficient.
- When transcripts **constitute the primary research data**, for example, in linguistic, legal, or psychological studies, **literal transcription** is recommended.

Even in cases requiring detailed transcripts, **automated transcription** can still be used as a starting point, provided that the researcher carefully reviews and corrects the output to ensure accuracy.

## Access

Amberscript offers both automated transcription and transcription carried out by professional transcribers. Automated transcription is available through prepaid credits per hour of audio. For verbatim or literal transcription by transcribers, a quote must be requested in advance.

For more information about cost breakdowns, see: [Transcription at VU](https://vu.nl/en/about-vu/divisions/university-library/more-about/transcription-at-vu-for-audio-and-video-files).
